⌚60+ Days super battery life - M2 is equipped with a 100% pure cobalt battery (military grade), the battery capacity is as high as 380mAh ensuring optimal performance and longevity which is twice as much as that of conventional smart watches. With 30-minute charge, you can have enough battery backup to last for up to 3 days.

⌚24H Health monitoring /70 Exercise modes - AMAZTIM M2 collects the health data with its advanced real-time monitoring function. The fitness watch helps you monitor All-day sleep, 24-hour heart rate, and real-time blood pressure and blood oxygen. reviews.title: Easy to set up, very accurate, keeps me updated with many aspect of my health
reviews.text:
I really appreciate this watch and the features it has. First off, the smart watch was very easy to pair to my phone. I have an Android phone, and it only took a few minutes to pair the watch with my phone.
First enable Bluetooth and find the device listed as "C20"

Next, download the app that's shown in the QR code. Once you have the watch paired via Bluetooth to your smart phone and the app is downloaded, your watch ready to go, and should be syncing to your smart phone. The connection has been great thus far; it hasn't disconnected/lost connection to my phone.

As someone who has suffers from pulmonary lung disease, I was very excited when I found out this smart watch has the capability to track my oxygen saturation, heart rate, and blood pressure. In addition, you don't have to do anything, the watch will automatically to measurements periodically throughout the day.

It's helping me monitor and compare results over a few days. I suggest authorizing Google Fit so your hearth history is accessible when it. For me, it helps to have a history when I meet with my pulmonologist.

The watch has great standby battery life, and it's light weight. After m three days it's only dipped minimally in available battery percentage. I highly recommend this!

**Updated**

So it's been several days now since I first posted the review and I am amazed how little the battery has drained. Needless to say this smartwatch has fantastic standby battery life; especially considering it's constantly looking for/measuring heart rate. As you can see from the photos the readings are very accurate. I own a pulse oximeter and a blood pressure monitor to keep my pulmonary hypertension in check. My resting heart rate is 95 bpm, which may seem high, but the reading is accurate and normal which makes this product more the reassuring.

reviews.title: Great Watch!
reviews.text: Ok, I have had fitness watches from Samsung to Fitbit to Garmin and this one is the best....especially for the price.  First thing since turning 60 I need a watch I can read in the sunlight.  This has a brightness level I actually do not have to turn it all the way up and the workout data is big enough to read. I think all the health data is correct, but do not trust the Blood Pressure readings because of the science behind it. Actually do not trust any BP on a fitness watch.  There is a lot of customization you can do......I recommend a custom watch face along with setting up quick view on a schedule. I am now going to see if I can get a spare charging cord,

reviews.title: Cheap, Inaccurate, but Cheap
reviews.text:
What works, the clock.
What doesn't work, everything else.
Bought in August, really tried to give it a chance. I knew for the price it was a gamble.

reviews.title: Step count
reviews.text:
Like the look, heft, easy set up.

But I question the accuracy since driving 1 miles and it's counted 30 steps, this is AFTER it logged 82 steps during at my desk.

3 month update. Battery life is still excellent however the watch has stopped connecting to the app

6 month update: Batter life is excellent, I notice it has started to decline, but that could also be heavier use. Step count still seems a bit wonky, but not egregiously off. The Strap is starting to break at the clasp and I will need to replace as soon as I'm able to find a replacement.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Having used different smart watches was looking forward to this one. But it disappointed me. Accuracy on everything was off compared to reality. Other smart watches for less were more accurate. I returned said watch.

reviews.title: It’s ok but not what I expected!
reviews.text: It’s ok but not what I expected!

reviews.title: Very stylish
reviews.text: Just what I was looking for and very stylish.

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: It’s ok
reviews.text: The oxygen part of the watch is not accurate.

reviews.title: So glad I found this one!
reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

EASILY CUSTOMIZABLE — Available in a range of sizes and colors, with dozens of bands to choose from and watch faces with complications tailored to whatever you’re into.

HEALTH AND SAFETY FEATURES — Get help when you need it with Crash Detection, Fall Detection, and Emergency SOS. Get deep insights into your health, including notifications if you have an irregular rhythm or an unusually high or low heart rate.

SIMPLY COMPATIBLE — It works seamlessly with your Apple devices and services. Unlock your Mac automatically. Find your devices with a tap. Pay and send money with Apple Pay. Apple Watch requires an iPhone 8 or later with the latest iOS version.

APPS ON YOUR WRIST — With access to tens of thousands of apps on the App Store right on your wrist, Apple Watch is the perfect personal device for whatever you love to do.

A POWERFUL FITNESS PARTNER — The enhanced Workout app gives you new ways to train and advanced metrics for more information about your workout performance. And Apple Watch comes with three months of Apple Fitness+ free.
STAY CONNECTED — Send a text, make a call, listen to music and podcasts, use Siri, or call for help with Emergency SOS. Apple Watch SE (GPS) works with your iPhone or Wi-Fi to keep you connected.

APPLECARE — Every Apple Watch comes with one year of hardware repair coverage through its limited warranty and up to 90 days of complimentary technical support. Get AppleCare+ to extend your coverage and reduce the stress and cost of unexpected repairs.
LEGAL DISCLAIMERS — This is a summary of the main product features. See below to learn more.

The smartwatch comes with 2 straps, which means you don't need to buy replacements. The strap is very easy to install and remove.

reviews.title: Love this watch
reviews.text: Nice looking watch with accurate steps. I love the gps feature where it shows your walk on a true map on the app. Nice visual. Heart rate is very accurate as well. Haven't used music feature as much as I thought I would. Garmin makes a nice watch. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Heart rate way off
reviews.text:
The main thing I bought this for is to monitor heart rate while hiking. Its reading is way off for me - as confirmed by a pulse ox and manually counting. The watch kept telling me my heart rate was over 100 just around the house. I have nsvt but my heart rate generally runs low. So I'm very sensitive when I have palpitations - when this watch kept saying my heart rate was over 95 I wasnt buying it. That brings into question any other readings or parameters.

I find the watch annoying and frustrating to use. The watch face does not interact very well for me. I'm constantly trying to tap it and it wont register so then the screen shuts off. Another annoying thing is it tells you to start moving around when I'm doing housework. I guess you can shut that off but I've really lost interest. Hopefully Garmin can offer some fix for the reading since its past the return date.

The positives - watch is comfortable and I don't wear jewelry. I forget its on. Its a large size so its readable if you have vision issues. I've had no problem with that.

reviews.title: Lightweight and perfect for small wrists
reviews.text: This is a great lightweight fitness tracker.  It has about a 4 day battery life and does a quick recharge.  It has a little learning curve for the activity tracking but it is right up there with my Apple Watch.

reviews.title: Exelente
reviews.text: La bateria me dura 14 dias. Pantalla se ve perfectamente bajo el sol. La app connect trabaja muy bien, en la tienda puedo descargar pantallas para el reloj. Resto de las actividades ok. Lo recomiendo

reviews.title: Large Screen-Bright White Band
reviews.text: I like everything about this except that I didn’t realize that the frame and the band would be a Brilliant white! There is a golden strip surrounding the watch face.

reviews.title: Nice feel. but easily broken
reviews.text: I ordered this very inexpensive band knowing the quality would not be the best. I was surprised how comfortable the band was and thought it was a nice upgrade over the base Ionic Bands. However after wearing it for a few weeks, when putting it back on after charging, the connection to the latch broke and its not repairable. Its a nice feeling material but this broke way to easily and after only a few weeks of use is rather disappointing.

reviews.text: Had it for about a month and a half broken already

reviews.title: Great Buy
reviews.text: Love the fit and security of this product. It’s strong, durable, and better than others I’ve had before. I will definitely use this seller and buy this product again in the future.

reviews.title: Junk is being nice
reviews.text: The worst ionic band so far. The mechanism that attaches the band to the watch broke within 1 month thankfully I can return to Amazon and don't have to deal with manufacturer.

title: Garmin Forerunner 245, GPS Running Smartwatch with Advanced Dynamics, Berry
description: You do the running. Forerunner 245 does the thinking. This GPS smartwatch doesn’t just tell you where you ran. It tracks your stats, crunches the numbers and gets to know all about your performance, your running form, your training history and even your goals. All you need to focus on is putting one foot in front of the other.Bezel Material: fiber-reinforced polymer.
features: GPS running smartwatch with advanced training features
Battery life: up to 7 days in smartwatch mode and up to 24 hours in GPS mode
Get free adaptive training plans from Garmin Coach, or create your own custom workouts on our Garmin Connect online fitness community

Provides advanced running dynamics, including ground contact time balance, stride length, vertical ratio and more (When used with Running Dynamics Pod or HRM-Run or HRM-Tri monitors (sold separately))
Safety and tracking features include incident detection (during select activities) which sends your real-time location to emergency contacts through your paired compatible smartphone
Customize with free watch faces, apps and more from our Connect IQ store

reviews.title: great looking watch and great for running and biking
reviews.text:
I really like this watch. I replaced and old forerunner. (many generation old) It feels great and looks good since you can download and tailor the watch faces. I have downloaded music to it using MediaMonkey and it worked fine. So far, synching with earbuds Jaybird Tarah Pro and Vistas, has been good. The availability of watch faces and activity widgets is nice.
But
Be warned, it takes a little bit to set it up and understand how to understand how to download and update activity widgets. Updating of watch faces isn't too bad. Be patient and read the user guide, there is a lot of functionality you should be aware of whether you use them or not.

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text:
Great, does all of the stuff running nerds love. Spotify is a hassle to sync when i want to adjust my playlists. This is especially annoying for podcast listeners such as myself. For this reason, I could have gone without the music upgrade and just used my phone but I still use the music feature a good amount for my shorter speed runs where I need the running jams!

But obviously I didn’t take a star because Garmin is the GOAT

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband, an avid runner, for our anniversary. He is super into tracking, numbers, stats, etc. And he loves this watch! He previously had the Forerunner 235 Garmin but it was probably close to five years old. He had done alot of research on this one verses the newer models and this one still came out ahead and did not disappoint!

reviews.title: great fitness watch!
reviews.text: if you are looking for a smart watch, then this is not for you. however, if you want a fitness watch, then get this one! the gps tracking is great and accurate! it takes a little bit to get used to the buttons and where all the settings are, but i love that i can customize it to do just what i want in terms of display, audio outputs, etc. the band is comfortable and the watch face is a good size to see my stats while working out without being overly cumbersome (i'm a small 5'0" female). i love that it can play my spotify playlists! the only thing of note is that it will not be able to get any data such as weather, sms notifications, calendar updates, emails, phone calls, or send emergency assistance notices unless you have your phone with you and connected via bluetooth. the point of this watch for me was to keep my phone at home so those features don't work during my run, but it does do the fitness tracking and play previously setup spotify playlists, so it's exactly what i want and need. i don't use it for sleep tracking since i only wear it during workouts. love the heart rate monitoring too!

reviews.title: Sleep Tracking and Incoming Call Notification (on android) are EPIC failures
reviews.text:
Overwhelming 5 start reviews really make me skeptical that the reviews themselves are genuine....
Overall the watch is not bad but really needs functional work. The Android Dev/QA team should have some re-training/structuring after releasing this to the general public...
Sleep tracking shows me as sleeping if I am in bed relaxed watching tv during sleep hours, making it useless. I only receive an incoming call notification on the watch if my phone screen is unlocked...
missed calls come in every time..

no problems with GPS, Battery Life, Music , Bluetooth, feeds to 3rd party athletic tracking apps. all those work fine.

Not sure I would buy this again.

reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:1dfba93f62e565c5c393181e1d06cded]] What I liked about the ITOUCH EXPLORER 3 SMART WATCH is the very accurate way it keeps track of your health it’s designed with your health in mind it’s easy to use and very easy to setup and stylish and sturdy comfortable wrist band and the battery life is fantastic easy to read dial and it’s a good value for your money it makes a great gift

reviews.title: Great looking watch, battery life isn't what I expected
reviews.text: The major feature that seems missing from this watch is sleep tracking.  Reviews of the watch point to the limited battery life of the watch, and not being enough to track though an average night's sleep.

reviews.title: Measure your wrist before you buy.
reviews.text:
The blue silicone band was too small to go around my wrist. I called Skagen and they offered to sell me a bigger band. I told them I shouldn't have to pay extra for the watch to go on my wrist. They said since I bought it from Amazon there was nothing they could do. So sadly, this is getting returned.
I've worn plenty of watches before, and I don't have a fatty wrist. My wrist is 8 inches in circumference, which is average for a male.
The interesting thing about the size as I inspected and measured the watch, is that if you measure the watch laid out flat, 8 inches goes to the second to last notch, and it should fit fine. BUT -- the watch body itself obviously does not bend, which means in actual wear when the watch goes AROUND your wrist, you need an additional 3/4 to 1 inch for it to work.
It's basically like that one gallon jug I bought at Walmart, I got it home to make a drink mix in it just to realize that yes, it held one gallon, if you filled it to the absolute brim, at which point putting in the drink mix made it spill.
If your wrist is greater than 7 inches in circumference, I would not buy this watch.

reviews.title: Not a good buy
reviews.text: The 1st one I got "went crazy" and started just cycling thru the various screens on the watch.  I returned it and got a replacement because we all know that "stuff happens".  I got the replacement and it seemed to be working well until day 28, when it stopped connecting with the app at all, even though I installed and uninstalled it several times as the troubleshooting recommended.  Then it started draining my battery dry and that meant it needed to be returned.  Don't waste your money on this one unless you have LOTS of time to fiddle with it!

reviews.title: I wanted to like this product...
reviews.text:
I wanted a simple activity tracker and this looked like it was perfect. There are a number of things that worked well, battery life, simplicity, matched my needs... However, the accuracy is non existent. I was at the gym working out vigorously and my heart rate was 50 and dropping. I checked it with another app on my phone and I was approaching 140. When I am at rest it creeps into the 90's. by the way, I have entered all profile info. The steps are also way off as I tested against an app on my phone.

I tried but its not worth the effort.
